target.name: Kairos Power
target.founder: Mike Laufer, Edward Blandford, Per Peterson

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: TerraPower
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $4B+

data.narrative:
Kairos Power, a late-stage nuclear energy company, has raised over $500M and carries a current post-money valuation of $1.6B. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 10 comparable companies—3 public and 7 private—operating in the advanced nuclear sector, including TerraPower, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Oklo, and Zap Energy, with public comparables trading under tickers OKLO, NNE, and SMR.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of late-stage financings, implies a forward valuation of $1.9B from the $1.6B base. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector comparables yields $1.8B; the peer universe spans $1.0B to $4.0B, with TerraPower at the high end ($4.0B) and Zap Energy at the low end ($1.0B). Both methods are applicable given Kairos' stage and sector positioning.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $1.5B to $2.2B, with a median of $1.8B. This range sits tightly clustered around the current $1.6B valuation, suggesting modest upside potential of 12% to the median case. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway risk, capital intensity of commercialization, and further public market comps as the sector scales.
